protocol.name: treeland_wallpaper_v1
protocol.copyright: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2025 UnionTech Software Technology Co., Ltd. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

interface.name: treeland_wallpaper_manager_v1
interface.description: The treeland_wallpaper_manager_v1 interface is a global object used to create wallpaper objects for specific outputs.

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroy this treeland_wallpaper_manager_v1 object.
Destroying a bound treeland_wallpaper_manager_v1 object while there are wallpapers still alive created by this treeland_wallpaper_manager_v1 object instance is illegal and will result in a protocol error.

request.name: get_treeland_wallpaper

description.summary: Create a wallpaper object for an output.

request.description:
Creates a treeland_wallpaper_v1 object bound to the specified wl_output.
The compositor may enforce that only one wallpaper exists for a given (output, role) combination.
When a surface is associated, the compositor will track the workspace the surface belongs to. Whenever the workspace changes, the compositor may notify the client of the current wallpaper file path used by that workspace.
Note: The treeland_wallpaper_v1 object must be destroyed before the associated wl_surface is destroyed.

arg.name: surface
arg.summary: The surface to associate with this wallpaper object, or null to remove the association

interface.name: treeland_wallpaper_v1

description.summary: Wallpaper object interface for setting and managing wallpaper content

interface.description:
The treeland_wallpaper_v1 interface represents a wallpaper instance created by the compositor for a specific output, role.
The wallpaper source provided by the client is stored and managed on the compositor side. Once a wallpaper source has been set, the compositor may cache, reuse, or persist the source independently of the client.

entry.name: already_used
entry.summary: wallpaper file has already been used and is cached on the wallpaper side

entry.name: invalid_source
entry.summary: The specified wallpaper source is invalid, unsupported, or failed to load.

entry.name: permission_denied
entry.summary: Permission denied when opening the specified wallpaper source

entry.name: image
entry.summary: The wallpaper source is an image

entry.name: video
entry.summary: The wallpaper source is a video

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroys the treeland_wallpaper_v1 object and releases all resources associated with it on the compositor side.
After calling this request, the client must not use the wallpaper object anymore.

request.name: set_image_source
request.description:
Sets an image as the wallpaper source, apply to the current wl_output and the active workspace. except for lockscreen wallpapers, only one wallpaper can be set per wl_output. Supported formats:
- BMP (Windows Bitmap) - GIF (Graphic Interchange Format) - JPG / JPEG (Joint Photographic Experts Group) - PNG (Portable Network Graphics) - PBM (Portable Bitmap) - PGM (Portable Graymap) - PPM (Portable Pixmap) - XBM (X11 Bitmap) - XPM (X11 Pixmap)
The compositor will attempt to load and display the specified image file. If the file cannot be accessed, decoded, or is in an unsupported format, the compositor will emit a failed event with an appropriate error code.
Security concern: This protocol accepts file_source such as /usr/share/wallpapers/xxx or /var/cache/wallpapers/xxxx provided by the client; the compositor will perform file verification.

arg.name: file_source
arg.summary: Path to the image file to be used as the wallpaper

request.name: set_video_source
request.description:
Sets a video file as the wallpaper source, apply to the current wl_output and the all workspace. except for lockscreen wallpapers, only one wallpaper can be set per wl_output. Supported formats:
- MP4 - MKV (Matroska) - WebM - AVI - MOV - FLV - MPEG-TS - OGG
The compositor is responsible for decoding and presenting the video. Playback behavior such as looping, synchronization, and performance characteristics are compositor-defined.
If the video source cannot be opened or decoded, a failed event will be emitted.
Security concern: This protocol accepts file_source such as /usr/share/wallpapers/xxx or /var/cache/wallpapers/xxxx provided by the client; the compositor will perform file verification.

arg.name: file_source
arg.summary: Path to the video file to be used as the wallpaper

event.name: failed
event.description:
This event is emitted when a wallpaper-related request fails.
The failure may occur during source loading, validation, or runtime setup. The error code provides additional information about the cause of the failure.
Possible error values include: - already_used: The wallpaper source is already configured. This is not a fatal error and serves as a notification to the client. - invalid_source: The specified wallpaper source is invalid, unsupported, or could not be processed. This is a fatal error and the client should verify the source before retrying. - Permission denied, check file permissions.

arg.name: error
arg.summary: The error code indicating the failure reason

event.name: changed

description.summary: Indicates that the wallpaper source has changed

event.description:
This event is emitted by the compositor to notify the client that the wallpaper source associated with this treeland_wallpaper_v1 object has changed.
The event may be emitted during initial object setup or when the wallpaper is changed due to external factors not initiated by this client, such as compositor policy decisions, workspace switches (except for lockscreen wallpapers) that affect the active wallpaper, or other system components.
Because the desktop wallpaper is designed to support different workspaces. if the client is a surface, changes to its displayed workspace will also send (reference set_reference_surface).
The event provides the current wallpaper source type and the corresponding source file path.

arg.name: source_type
arg.summary: The type of the current wallpaper source

arg.name: file_source
arg.summary: The file path of the current wallpaper source

interface.name: treeland_wallpaper_produce_v1
interface.description:
The treeland_wallpaper_produce_v1 interface is a global object exposed by the compositor. It allows clients to create and manage wallpaper surface objects associated with a wl_surface.
A wallpaper producer represents a source of wallpaper content, such as an image file or a dynamic media source. The compositor may notify the client about wallpaper lifecycle changes through events on this object.
This interface is a singleton. At most one client may bind to treeland_wallpaper_produce_v1 at any given time.

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroy this treeland_wallpaper_produce_v1 object.
Destroying a bound treeland_wallpaper_produce_v1 object while there are wallpaper surfaces still alive created by this treeland_wallpaper_produce_v1 object instance is illegal and will result in a protocol error.

request.name: get_treeland_wallpaper_surface
request.description:
Create a treeland_wallpaper_surface_v1 object and associate it with the given wl_surface.
The provided surface will be used exclusively for displaying the wallpaper content. The client must not use the surface for any other role while it is assigned as a wallpaper surface.
The file_source argument specifies the initial wallpaper source path. Its interpretation depends on the wallpaper source type selected by the compositor.

arg.name: id
arg.summary: The newly created wallpaper surface object

arg.name: surface
arg.summary: The surface used to present the wallpaper content

arg.name: file_source
arg.summary: The file path of the initial wallpaper source

event.name: add

description.summary: Notify the client of a new wallpaper source

event.description:
This event is sent by the compositor when a new wallpaper source is added or becomes active.
The client may use this information to update its internal state or user interface. The meaning of the file_source argument depends on the reported source_type.

arg.name: source_type
arg.summary: The type of the wallpaper source

arg.name: file_source
arg.summary: The file path or identifier of the wallpaper source

event.name: removed

description.summary: Notify that a wallpaper surface has been removed

event.description:
This event indicates that the given treeland_wallpaper_surface_v1 object is no longer valid.
After receiving this event, the client should destroy the wallpaper surface object and must not send any further requests to it.

arg.name: file_source
arg.summary: The file path or identifier of the wallpaper source

interface.name: treeland_wallpaper_surface_v1
interface.description:
The treeland_wallpaper_surface_v1 interface controls the behavior of a wallpaper surface.
A wallpaper surface is bound to exactly one wl_surface and defines how that surface should be sized, positioned, and synchronized with the compositor's wallpaper management logic.

entry.name: invalid_source
entry.summary: The specified wallpaper source is invalid, unsupported, or failed to load.

entry.name: permission_denied
entry.summary: Permission denied when opening the specified wallpaper source

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroy the wallpaper surface object and release its association with the underlying wl_surface.
After calling this request, the wallpaper surface object becomes invalid and must not be used again.
This request should only be sent when the client intends to permanently stop using the wallpaper surface, such as when the client is shutting down or after receiving the treeland_wallpaper_produce_v1.removed event for this surface.

request.name: source_failed
request.description:
Reports to the compositor that a wallpaper-related operation has failed on the client side.
This request may be sent by the client when it fails to open, load, or otherwise process the configured wallpaper source. The compositor may use this information for diagnostics, policy decisions, or to adjust internal state.
Possible error values include: - invalid_source: The specified wallpaper source is invalid, unsupported, or could not be processed. This is a fatal error and indicates that the source should be verified before retrying. - permission_denied: The client does not have permission to access the specified wallpaper source.

arg.name: error
arg.summary: The error code indicating the failure reason

event.name: position
event.description:
This event provides a position value associated with the wallpaper.
The semantic meaning of the position value is compositor-defined. It may represent a scroll offset, animation progress, or playback position. The value is expressed as a fixed-point number[0, 1.0].

arg.name: position
arg.summary: Position or progress value in fixed-point format

event.name: pause
event.description:
This event instructs the client to pause wallpaper updates or animations.
After receiving this event, the client should stop advancing any time-based or animated wallpaper content until a play event is received.

event.name: play
event.description: This event instructs the client to resume wallpaper updates or animations after a pause.
